title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Frankie continues her investigation into the identity of the man who fell off the cliff in Machabert. Through a conversation with Sylvia Bassington-French, Frankie learns the man's name was Allen Castles, a Canadian naturalist, explorer, and big-game hunter who had once traveled with a millionaire named John Savage. Frankie suspects Castles is the deceased man because he had no close relatives in the country, meaning his disappearance would go unnoticed. She recalls that the Rivingtons once brought Castles to lunch, providing a potential lead, and she contemplates a theory that Castles may have been killed while investigating a person named Evans or a drug cartel.

That evening, Dr. Nicholson and his wife, Moira, arrive for dinner. Frankie observes that Dr. Nicholson is a tall man with light blue eyes who drives a deep blue Talbot, noting the coincidence that Castles was also Canadian and drove a similar car. During dinner, Henry Basington-French appears subdued and anxious, while Moira seems to watch her husband with a mixture of attention and potential fear. Dr. Nicholson engages Frankie in a cryptic conversation about "cultivating" bacteria in the countryside, which leaves her feeling unsettled. He then interrogates her regarding her recent car accident, using observations from a messenger boy named Rives to suggest that the doctor who assisted her must have taken an unusual route. Frankie finds Dr. Nicholson's intense focus on details suspicious and perceives him as a cunning individual.
